what_it_does: Defines the prescriptive constitution for the nodes module: what node types are, how each is a self-contained C2 definition, how new types self-register on drop-in, the VOLATILE rule for nodes that read mutable external truth, the extension path (including subgraph-as-node), and the seams/prohibitions that keep node definitions separate from runtime, UI, and tools.
observations: Nodes are grouped into three kinds: process, content/asset, and presentation.
Every node-type is one self-contained C2 declaration with ports, config_schema, output_schema, render_set, and inspector_schema.
Drop-in self-registration makes a node queryable in the type-graph and visible in the palette via /object_info without changing runtime, UI, or tools.
AI nodes reuse fabric/ guards; content nodes hold a content address, never inline bytes.
Live-state nodes that read mutable truth must declare module-level VOLATILE=True or the memo gate will serve stale results forever.
A new node-type is added as a new folder nodes/<name>/ with its definition and a tiny constitution.
Subgraph-as-node (HDA) lets a saved canvas's promoted ports/config become a new node type.
The live complete registry is auto-maintained in the Company Map and must not be duplicated here.
Examples include process nodes (uppercase, titlecase, wordcount, join, llm, ask, pair), content nodes (constant, codebase, portal), and live-state/special nodes (model_of_tim, rhm_mode).
rhm_mode is a mode-as-node and carries the voice_enabled toggle read by Suite.voice_enabled().
Negative constraints: never edit runtime/ or canvas/ to add a type, never write per-type frontend code, never inline large content, never ship a mutable-truth node without VOLATILE=True.
standouts: The VOLATILE=True rule is framed as a hard correctness guard against silent stale memoization.
Drop-in self-registration and the explicit prohibition on per-type frontend code make node addition a backend-only concern.
Subgraph-as-node (HDA) is offered as a first-class extension path.
A mode and a feature toggle (rhm_mode, voice_enabled) are modeled as node types rather than configuration flags.
conventions: Follows the vault constitution note structure with frontmatter fields (type, register, module, aliases, tags, governs, relates-to, status) and sections for Is, Guarantees, Where new things go, To extend, Seam, Never, What's in here, Relates to, Read next.
Uses [[wikilink]] references to related constitutions and maps.
Avoids duplicating the live registry, deferring to Company Map per Vault Conventions.
States rules as guarantees and negative constraints ('Must', 'Never').
concerns: The 'Never' section explicitly warns that omitting VOLATILE causes silent staleness, suggesting this failure mode is known and possibly easy to miss.
The rhm_mode node combines a presence dial with a voice feature toggle, which may conflate two responsibilities in one node type.
The 'What's in here' list is intentionally partial and relies entirely on an external file for the source of truth.
The distinction between content/asset and presentation kinds is asserted but only sparsely exemplified.
notes: This is the prescriptive constitution for the node library, centered on C2 self-registration, the VOLATILE correctness rule, and keeping node definitions decoupled from runtime and canvas code.
questions: What is the full C2 schema beyond the five listed fields?
How is the VOLATILE=True requirement enforced — static lint, runtime check, or convention?
What is the exact layout and content of the 'tiny constitution' in a new node folder?
How does the type-graph query and /object_info endpoint work mechanically?
What does HDA stand for and how are promoted ports/config extracted from a saved canvas?
What does model_of_tim contain and how is it kept current?
How does rhm_mode interact with Suite.voice_enabled() at the code level?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to govern the nodes module, and the content matches that by defining node taxonomy, the C2 contract, self-registration, extension paths, seams, and prohibitions.
apparent_themes: separation of node definitions from runtime and UI
intention_signals: It aims to make the node library a self-extending catalog of typed, composable units that can be added without touching core systems, while preventing silent correctness bugs from caching mutable external state.
novelty: The VOLATILE flag as a module-level correctness contract tied to memoization, and the treatment of a mode dial plus a voice toggle as first-class node types.
contribution: It contributes a prescriptive governance layer that defines how node types are declared, registered, extended, and constrained, including the VOLATILE correctness rule and the separation between node definitions and runtime/canvas/rendering systems.
summary_for_embedding: Constitution for the nodes module describing C2 self-registering node types, process/content/presentation taxonomy, the VOLATILE rule for mutable-truth nodes, drop-in extension, and prohibitions on per-type frontend changes.
intention_for_embedding: To govern how node types are added and maintained so the library remains self-extending, queryable, and safe from stale memoization without modifying core runtime or UI code.
